titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Daggett Family Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Robert Bartels, Diane E. Kaplan and William E. Brown, Jr.

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday June 27, 2023 at 07:01
Finding aid revision description not supplied.
Finding aid revised and updated by Robert Bartels

did.abstract: The collection consists of correspondence, legal and financial documents, writings, photographs and ephemera, and research materials and genealogies pertaining to the Daggett family.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gifts from various sources, 1931-1975, purchases from several dealers, 1944-1954, and gifts from Beatrice S. Bartlett, 2019-2021.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Daggett Family Papers (MS 1382). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Additional Henry Daggett (1741-1830) material is available in the Hooker Family Papers (MS 289). Additional Naphtali Daggett material is available in the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Additional David Daggett material is available in the David Daggett Papers (MS 162).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of correspondence, legal and financial documents, writings, photographs and ephemera, and research materials and genealogies pertaining to the Daggett family.

Includes the correspondence of David Daggett, his wife Wealthy Ann Munson Daggett, his sons Leonard Augustus Daggett and Rev. Oliver Ellsworth Daggett, and his grandson Dr. David Lewis Daggett. Also included is correspondence between Dr. David Lewis Daggett and his wife, Margaret Donaldson Gibbons Daggett, their son Leonard Mayhew Daggett, and Margaret's sister Sarah Ellen Gibbons Thompson. Business records include business, legal, and financial records from several members of the Daggett family, including a group of deeds belonging to David Daggett. Also contains estate records and property inventories relating to the deaths of Daggett family members. Research materials consist of biographical information, family histories, obituaries, and geneologies, the majority being compiled by Beatrice S. Bartlett. Included in smaller quantities are papers of Alfred, Ebenezer, Henry (1741-1830), Henry (1758-1843), Horace, Naphtali, and William Daggett. Napthali, who was president of Yale College (1766-1777), and Henry (1758-1843) were father and son; the relationships of other family members is distant or unclear.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection are in the public domain. There are no restrictions on use. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The material is arranged into five series: correspondence, business and legal records, writings, photographs and ephemera, and family research materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A letter of Henry Daggett's (1741-1830) to his wife while he was serving in the Connecticut State Legislature.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Donated to the library by Mrs. Henry Daggett Hooker, 1931.

otherfindaid.head: Other Finding Aids
otherfindaid.p: Similar material is included in the Hooker Family papers (MS 289).

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Letters were originally bundled together; during processing were unbuldled and foldered by archivist.

processinfo.p: Letters were originally bundled together; during processing were unbuldled and foldered by archivist.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Letters were originally bundled together; during processing were unbuldled and foldered by archivist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The majority of Margaret Donaldson Daggett's correspondence is to her mother, Rebecca Donaldson Gibbons, or her husband, Dr. David L. Daggett.

Certificate of membership to the American Antiquarian Society
Certificate of membership to the Bunker Hill Monument Association

acqinfo.p: The document was purchased from Hurwitz in 1945.

did.unittitle: Certificate of appointment as notary public for the state of Connecticut, signed by Roger S. Baldwin
Daggett, Margaret Donaldson Gibbons, receipt book
Daggett, Susan Elizabeth, death and estate
Daggett, David Lewis, compositions during sophomore and junior year
Daggett, Leonard Augustus, personal papers and speeches

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes sermon on the death of Yale President Thomas Clap and another given in the college chapel.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Donated by Mrs. F. Allen Sherk, 1975.

did.unittitle: Literary Lapses , by Henry W. Farnam
"Political Phases of the Labor Question in the United States," essay entry for John A. Porter University Prize, unidentified author

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note on inside reads, "Book may have belonged to Ellsworth Daggett or his sister Susan Elizabeth Daggett (as it seems to have many of her friends, perhaps from Vassar)".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note on inside reads, "This album probably belonged to Oliver Ellsworth Daggett, Yale Class of 1828".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Possibly belonged to either Dr. David L. Daggett or his son, Leonard Mayhew Daggett.

did.unittitle: Atwater, Mrs. Timothy, and daughter Jeannette Atwater

accessrestrict.p: Access restricted pending conservation housing, June 2023.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: David Lewis Daggett (February 8th, 1792-October 9th, 1810) was the son of David Daggett and the brother of Leonard Augustus Daggett.

did.unittitle: A guide to rapid and accurate computation by professor W. Hutchings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscribed on medals are "Rebecca Donaldson Beach" and "Lt. R. V. Beach", likely Rodman Vernon Beach (1865-1898).

did.unittitle: Order of exercises for ordination of Reverend Sereno E. Dwight, Park Street Church, Boston
Sketch by Washington Townsend, presented to Mrs. Gibbons
Princeton and Harvard degrees of Naphtali Daggett
